purpose: Decision-state contract for public and adjacent authenticated app and marketing surfaces. It records which UI and actions are visible, gated, local, native, web, or external without duplicating the existing screen, route, async-state, or booking-policy inventories.
coveragePolicy: Each surface declares only dimensions that change its behavior and a targeted set of meaningful configurations. Loading, empty, error, offline, permission, mutation, theme, and accessibility remain orthogonal in the design-parity state matrix; event eligibility remains owned by the viewer availability resolver.

compositions.notes: The behavior configurations are evaluated within populated/readable content. The existing design-parity contract continues to own async, mutation, visual-theme, and accessibility variants.
The matrix consumes the resolver's outcome and does not restate age, invite, membership, cohort, capacity, waitlist, or run-preference policy calculations.
One fail-closed website resolver translates canonical authority and four independent capabilities into trust, claim, review-target, review-read, and review-write policy facts.

dimensions.description: Authentication lifecycle relevant to route and action gating.
The active native runtime. Consumer routes deny non-public listings while the host runtime may render an owner preview.
Signed-in consumer readiness used by booking and social feature gates.
Viewer relationship to the organizer represented by the listing.
Canonical native-app discovery visibility.
Whether native organizer lifecycle fields permit public browsing. Unavailable covers both lifecycle status archived and the legacy archived flag.
Canonical owner authority state, distinct from crawl origin and verification.
Canonical claim workflow state.
Canonical provenance confidence presented to viewers.
Whether a listing resolves to an app user profile that can support profile or message actions.
Canonical marketing-page publication state.
Whether the event is operated inside Catch or represented as an outbound source event.
Time and cancellation state that independently changes booking and review actions.
Resolved viewer-specific event eligibility, participation, and lifecycle result.
Availability of a map-safe event location and directions target.
Whether the generated listing can submit an ownership claim. This is independent from public review reads and writes.
Whether a verified canonical organizer or club backend target exists for public review reads and writes.
Whether published public reviews may be read for the listing.
Whether a public review may be submitted for the listing. A write never implies immediate publication.
Whether the organizer exposes at least one safe Instagram, telephone, or email contact action.
Whether booking is free, supported by the current native payment runtime, or currently unsupported.
Whether the website Firebase and App Check runtime required to authenticate and submit claims is available.
Whether the website Firebase and App Check runtime required to read remote reviews and submit moderated reviews is available.

dispositions.description: The element is absent for this configuration.
Visible information with no direct action.
Enabled action completed by the native app.
Enabled action completed on the Catch website.
Enabled action handed to an external URL or system app.
Enabled action persisted only in local browser or device state.
Action remains discoverable but requires authentication before continuing.
Action requires the appropriate signed-in profile readiness before continuing.
Visible action with an explanatory unavailable state.
The app route is accessible in this viewer state.
The private route redirects a guest into the authentication flow.
The private app route waits on the loading route until authentication or profile state resolves.
The signed-in app route redirects to the required booking- or social-profile completion step.
A completed viewer leaves the transient onboarding route and resumes the requested or default destination.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Explore is the logged-out app preset. Organizer cards expose authority, Catch events open natively, external events hand off to their source, and account-only controls stay absent until auth resolves signed in.

elements.description: Organizer discovery card.
Crawled, claimed, or verified provenance treatment.
Open organizer detail.
Follow an organizer, sign in first, or show the already-following state.
Open a Catch event detail route.
Open an external event source.
Open the signed-in viewer's saved events.
Filter discovery to joined events.
Search organizer and event supply.
elements.constantRationale: This discovery control is available whenever the populated Explore shell is rendered; permission, loading, empty, and error variants remain owned by the orthogonal state compositions.

elements.description: Open discovery filters that are independent of listing provenance.
elements.constantRationale: This discovery control is available whenever the populated Explore shell is rendered; permission, loading, empty, and error variants remain owned by the orthogonal state compositions.

contains: ExploreMapLauncherState hides until mapped supply is loaded

organizer.ownershipState.transferred: Transferred authority renders the same claimed trust treatment as claimed ownership in discovery.

organizer.claimState.claimPending: Claim-pending badge parity is verified by the shared authority resolver and Organizer Detail harness rows.

viewer.relationship.host: Explore is a consumer discovery surface; owned host preview behavior is tracked on Organizer Detail.

surfaces.coverageRationale: The map is publicly reachable and reuses Explore supply. Catch markers open native detail; external markers hand off to the source; hidden organizers cannot contribute Catch markers.

elements.description: Catch or external event map marker.
Open Catch event detail from a selected marker.
Open an external event source from a selected marker.
Limit map supply to the signed-in viewer's joined events.

contains: ExploreMapLauncherState hides until mapped supply is loaded
map view model preserves external event identity and coordinate

surfaces.coverageRationale: Organizer detail is guest-readable only for public consumer supply. It distinguishes authority, hides account data from guests, disables self-actions, and permits the host runtime to preview its own hidden listing.

elements.description: Whether the runtime may render this organizer.
Listing authority and verification treatment.
Sign in, join, or leave the organizer community.
Open the native organizer share sheet.
Toggle organizer push notifications for an active member.
Read event-scoped organizer review aggregates.
Open an app-backed host profile when one exists.
Message another organizer host when authenticated.
Open an organizer event.
Open a projected Instagram, telephone, or email contact target.
Owner management remains in the host workspace, not the public detail body.
elements.constantRationale: The host detail route is a read-only public-profile preview; management actions live in the dedicated host organizer workspace.

contains: loaded preview renders canonical content behind one boundary
DockBell retains toggle and pending semantics over icon chrome

viewer.profileReadiness.incomplete: Organizer membership does not require booking-profile fields; incomplete and booking-ready viewers share the same non-social messaging state.

organizer.ownershipState.transferred: Transferred ownership uses the same claimed trust and owner-action rules as claimed ownership.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Catch-native Event Detail is guest-readable only when its organizer is public. Saving, booking, review writing, and owner/self suppression vary by session, profile readiness, relationship, lifecycle, and resolved availability. External events never enter this route.

elements.description: Whether the runtime may render the event and its organizer.
Host-listing provenance treatment.
Open the native event share sheet.
Add an upcoming hosted or joined event to the system calendar.
Book, request, join a waitlist, or manage current participation.
Save the event in Catch.
Write an attendee review after eligibility unlocks.
Open the event location map.
Open the signed-up attendee companion plan.
Share an invite after the viewer has joined an upcoming event.
Open the owning organizer in the correct consumer or host route family.
Message the organizer host when the viewer is social-ready and not the host.

contains: derives eligible paid and paid-unsupported booking states
unlocks reviews only for attended users after the event ends

viewer.relationship.member: Organizer membership is resolved into event.availability; the relationship label does not independently change Event Detail actions.

organizer.ownershipState.transferred: Transferred ownership uses the same claimed trust treatment and owner suppression as claimed ownership.

organizer.claimState.claimPending: Claim-pending presentation is exercised on Organizer Detail; Event Detail consumes the same authority badge resolver.

surfaces.coverageRationale: The public location route uses the authority-gated Event Detail view model, then varies map and directions affordances by exact coordinate availability.

elements.description: Access to the event location route.
Pinned event map content.
Open directions in a system mapping app.

contains: event location map centers a pinned event and labels it
event location map keeps directions as an explicit action

viewer.relationship.member: Membership does not change a public event location once authority permits the route.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Saved Events is adjacent to public discovery but contains viewer-owned data and needs only an exact authentication guard.

elements.description: Authentication guard for saved viewer data.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Every consumer tab root other than Explore, plus adjacent viewer-owned history, remains absent from the guest shell and redirects direct guest links to sign-in.

elements.description: Authentication guard for private consumer roots and viewer history.

contains: authenticated app shell loads Chats and Profile branches

surfaces.coverageRationale: Companion plans are participation-sensitive attendee data. A guest must not inherit public access merely because the route sits below the organizer prefix.

elements.description: Authentication guard for attendee companion plans.
Authentication and profile-readiness route guard.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Filters and active event-matching routes require a social-ready profile in addition to authentication.

elements.description: Authentication and profile-readiness route guard.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Onboarding is itself the authenticated completion destination, so incomplete signed-in viewers may access it while guests may not.
Home exposes provenance-aware public organizer cards and listing navigation. Claim, save, and review actions belong to detail routes.

elements.description: Featured organizer discovery card.
Public listing authority treatment.
Open the generated organizer listing.

contains: deduplicates format suggestions and normalizes activity kinds

organizer.publishStatus.removed: Draft, QA, suppressed, and removed all fail the published-only public discovery selector.

organizer.claimState.claimPending: Claim-pending discovery uses the shared claim-in-review authority badge verified on Organizer Detail.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Search exposes provenance-aware filtering and listing navigation without requiring a website login. Claim, save, and review actions belong to detail routes.

elements.description: Organizer search result.

surfaces.coverageRationale: The canonical and legacy organizer listing families share claim, review, save, Catch-event, external-event, and provenance actions; they do not provide a standalone web Event Detail route.

elements.description: Whether the public route resolver exposes this listing.
Listing provenance and verification treatment.
Claim an eligible organizer page.
Authenticate and submit the embedded organizer claim form.
Open the founding-host fallback when the embedded claim runtime is unavailable.
Read published public and verified attendee review lanes.
Submit a public web review without app authentication; moderation may delay publication.
Navigate to the Catch event anchor and app-download path.
Open the external source event.
Persist a local browser save.
Share or copy the public organizer listing URL.

configurations.notes: Defensive stale-projection row: the schema forbids target-disabled review capabilities from being enabled, while runtime policy still proves target readiness wins if contradictory data is encountered.
Defensive stale-projection row: the schema forbids published plus suppressed, while runtime and static routing still deny it if contradictory data is encountered.

contains: persists organizer saves and exposes event-aware navigation
publishes configured reviews and refreshes the listing review query
builds eligible event cards with stable deep links

organizer.publishStatus.draft: All non-published values resolve to the same public not-found state.
organizer.publishStatus.suppressed: All non-published values resolve to the same public not-found state.
organizer.publishStatus.removed: All non-published values resolve to the same public not-found state.

organizer.ownershipState.transferred: Transferred ownership follows claimed ownership policy.

organizer.claimState.claimPending: Claim-pending uses the same non-claimable listing body with a claim-in-review badge.

surfaces.coverageRationale: Claim and lookup routes are the only marketing surfaces whose actions depend on website authentication as well as listing claim state and the independent claim-request capability.

elements.description: Authenticate the claimant on the website.
Claimant role, contact, and evidence form.
Submit the typed claim request.
Pending, already claimed, or unavailable status.

contains: returns an empty claim route to listing selection on submit
validates a preselected route before attempting an unconfigured write
submits the typed claim packet and refreshes both claim query families
blocks a listing claim before mutation when its public API is disabled

organizer.publishStatus.draft: All non-published listings are absent from claim lookup.
organizer.publishStatus.qa: All non-published listings are absent from claim lookup.
organizer.publishStatus.suppressed: All non-published listings are absent from claim lookup.

organizer.ownershipState.transferred: Transferred ownership is already owned and follows claimed ownership status.

organizer.claimState.suppressed: Suppressed listings are absent from public lookup.

absentSurfaces.reason: External events remain outbound rows and map pins. They must not enter the Catch-native Event Detail, booking, save, review, or companion flows.
Catch-native event booking remains an app action. Website event cards stay within organizer listings or hand off externally; there is no standalone web Event Detail route.
The website has Home organizer discovery and Organizer Search, but no route equivalent to the app's personalized Explore feed or map.
